steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
Both equations equal $y$, so set their right sides equal:
$$5x+4=5x^2+4$$

steps.title: Find the shared point
steps.content:
Subtract $5x+4$ from both sides:
$$0=5x^2-5x$$
$$0=5x(x-1)$$
The possible $x$-values make one factor equal $0$.

steps.title: Check the listed point
steps.content: Graph the two equations and choose the listed ordered pair at an intersection point. The listed intersection point is $(0,4)$. The answer is B.
